evidence: current main head independently resolved from openai/codex@main
current alpha head independently resolved from openai/codex@latest-alpha-cli

supervisorySessionController.summary: Requalify the current CUEstrap v2 supervisor against expanded hook lifecycle, permission/approval changes, instruction context, tool routing, and session identity before claiming compatibility.

idiomaticCueWorkbookHarness.summary: Update workbook MCP, code-mode, structured-result, and context-lifecycle qualification against current app, connector, and runtime projections.

critical.title: Hook ingress expanded beyond the current CUEstrap boundary
critical.summary: Both channels change hook metadata and add SessionEnd and context-limit behavior, while CUEstrap's closed ingress currently models only PreToolUse and PostToolUse.
critical.localContractImpact: Requalify supported hook events, common hook identity fields, end-of-session reduction, and context-spill behavior before claiming current Codex hook coverage.

critical.title: Permission, approval, and sandbox contracts changed
critical.summary: Approval response schemas and permission world-state paths changed on both channels against CUEstrap's fixed permission-mode vocabulary and fail-closed policy.
critical.localContractImpact: Requalify the CUE and Pydantic permission vocabulary, approval response projection, and sandbox admission rules against each channel independently.

critical.title: Instruction, skill, and context policy changed
critical.summary: AGENTS processing, environment-scoped skill loading, collaboration context, and realtime delegation changed across the current channels.
evidence: fatb4f/cuestrap@781801e6500bcef92169b8748ae82166bae56c88: .codex/AGENTS.md closes the repository phase and tool-use contract
critical.localContractImpact: Requalify repository instruction loading, phase isolation, environment-scoped skill selection, and collaboration/realtime context boundaries.

critical.title: Tool dispatch and MCP routing changed
critical.summary: MCP connection management, dynamic tools, code-mode values, and main thread-scoped MCP refresh changed while CUEstrap now closes target IDs and phase-specific routing.
evidence: fatb4f/cuestrap@781801e6500bcef92169b8748ae82166bae56c88: supervisory target IDs and routing are closed in contracts.cue, policy.py, and routing.py
critical.localContractImpact: Requalify canonical tool classification, target-ID routing, MCP refresh semantics, and pending-operation correlation before adopting either channel.

high.title: Tool-result, error, and additional-context semantics changed
high.summary: Dynamic tool responses, code-mode result values, hook context limits, and result projection changed against CUEstrap's returned/reported-error reducer and PostToolUse additionalContext response.
evidence: fatb4f/cuestrap@781801e6500bcef92169b8748ae82166bae56c88: Supervisor emits PostToolUse additionalContext and reduces returned|reported-error|not-dispatched|reducer-error
high.localContractImpact: Update and test result normalization, reported-error detection, context-size handling, and post-tool guidance serialization.

high.title: Session, thread, turn, and rollout lifecycle changed
high.summary: Thread and turn schemas changed on both channels; main additionally introduced inherited history paging and rollout-reference protection.
high.localContractImpact: Requalify session/run/attempt binding, turn continuity, transcript identity, and any replay assumptions used by the durable supervisory ledger.

high.title: Workbook-facing MCP and app projections changed
high.summary: Installed-app, app-read, connector runtime projection, MCP resources, and code-mode service behavior changed while CUEstrap added a workbook MCP server and constrained code-mode client.
evidence: fatb4f/cuestrap@781801e6500bcef92169b8748ae82166bae56c88: workbook_mcp_server.py and code_mode_client.py are current adapter surfaces
high.localContractImpact: Compare current MCP/app envelopes with workbook transaction, session resolution, structured observations, and browserless qualification behavior.

notes.title: Multi-agent ownership remains an explicit boundary
notes.summary: Agent spawning, role, notification, and realtime delegation changed while CUEstrap still binds one supervisory session/run/attempt and has no cross-agent ownership protocol.
notes.localContractImpact: Keep cross-agent ownership, delegated approvals, and attempt correlation unsupported until a separate contract is designed.

notes.title: Main and alpha remain independent qualification baselines
notes.summary: Main advanced 161 commits from its prior CUEstrap baseline; latest-alpha-cli diverged 150 commits ahead and one behind and now declares 0.145.0-alpha.29.
notes.localContractImpact: Preserve separate support claims, fixtures, and qualification evidence for main and latest-alpha-cli.
